entry.title: Phillies Trade Deadline Plans Just Changed
entry.summary:
The Phillies suffered a major blow with the loss of Brad Keller for the rest of the season, creating an even bigger need ahead of the MLB Trade Deadline. Alanna Rizzo, Todd Frazier and Erik Kratz discuss how Keller's injury changes Dave Dombrowski's deadline priorities, whether Philadelphia should prioritize bullpen help over a right-handed bat, and which trade targets could help the Phillies make another postseason run.

entry.title: How Aggressive Should The White Sox Be At The Trade Deadline?
entry.summary:
James Fegan of Sox Machine joins Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Scott Braun following the announcement that Sox Machine is officially joining the Foul Territory Network! We discuss the surprising first-place White Sox, why Chicago's turnaround happened sooner than expected, and what the club should do at the trade deadline. Plus, James breaks down the White Sox's biggest roster needs, whether Reid Detmers is a realistic trade target, and which young players could become the foundation of the franchise for years to come.

entry.title: MLB Insider Robert Murray Shares The Latest Trade Deadline Buzz
entry.summary:
MLB insider Robert Murray of FanSided joins Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Scott Braun to break down the latest trade deadline buzz around baseball. We discuss the Padres' plans with Mason Miller, the Brewers' trade for Lance McCullers Jr., which teams could become the biggest buyers and sellers, and why Tarik Skubal could still be the biggest name to watch before the deadline. Plus, Robert shares the latest on the Phillies' trade targets, the White Sox's surprising position as buyers, and a few under-the-radar players who could end up on the move.

entry.title: BREAKING: Reds Lock Up Chase Burns Long-Term
entry.summary:
Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Scott Braun break down Chase Burns' seven-year extension with the Reds and what it means for both sides. We discuss why more young pitchers are choosing long-term security over free agency, how Cincinnati has built one of baseball's best young rotations, and why the organization's investment in pitching development is paying off. Plus, we explain why this deal could become a blueprint for small-market teams moving forward.

entry.title: These MLB Teams Changed Our Expectations For The Second Half
entry.summary:
A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun discuss the biggest surprises heading into the second half of the MLB season. They break down the Red Sox incredible turnaround, debate whether Boston should be buyers at the trade deadline, and explain what's gone wrong for the Athletics after their stunning collapse. Plus, the guys share their thoughts on the first-place White Sox, the Cubs playoff chances, and several under-the-radar teams that could make noise after the All-Star break.

entry.title: Cole Hamels On The 2008 Phillies, Zack Wheeler & This Year's World Series Hopes
entry.summary:
Former MLB All-Star Cole Hamels joins A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun live from Philadelphia during MLB All-Star Week! Hamels reflects on the 2008 Phillies, explains why he still roots for the franchise, and shares behind-the-scenes stories from one of the greatest rotations in baseball. Plus, he weighs in on Zack Wheeler's All-Star snub, discusses how starting pitchers should be developed in today's game, and looks back on some unforgettable moments from his Phillies career.

entry.title: Ken Rosenthal On How MLB's Crowded Playoff Race Will Impact The Trade Deadline
entry.summary:
FT Senior Insider Ken Rosenthal breaks down how MLB's crowded playoff races could completely reshape this year's trade deadline. With 23 of 30 teams still within four games of a playoff spot entering the All-Star break, Ken explains why buyers and sellers are harder to identify than ever. Plus, he discusses what the Tigers should do with Tarik Skubal, why the Red Sox have changed the conversation, and how teams like the Astros, Blue Jays, Orioles, Diamondbacks and Padres could approach one of the most unpredictable deadlines in recent memory.

entry.title: 10x All-Star Chris Sale Shares Hilarious Stories From His White Sox Days With AJ
entry.summary:
Braves ace Chris Sale joins A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun live from MLB All-Star Week in Philadelphia! Sale reflects on earning his 10th All-Star selection, looks back on his first Midsummer Classic alongside legends like Derek Jeter, Justin Verlander and David Ortiz, and discusses what it means to still be pitching at an elite level. Plus, Sale shares his thoughts on Zack Wheeler's All-Star snub, reveals whether he still has another gear on the mound, and tells hilarious stories from his early days with A.J. in Chicago.

entry.title: Full Home Run Derby Preview: Predictions, Sleepers & New Rules
entry.summary:
A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun preview the 2026 Home Run Derby from Philadelphia and break down MLB's new format. The guys debate whether the changes will improve the event, discuss which hitters are best suited for Citizens Bank Park, and share their favorite picks to take home the trophy. Plus, they explain why Kyle Schwarber, Bryce Harper, Junior Caminero and Jac Caglianone could thrive under the new rules, while A.J. offers his own ideas to make the Derby even more exciting.

entry.title: Anthony Volpe Shuts Down False Report From Michael Kay
entry.summary:
Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Alanna Rizzo react to Anthony Volpe shutting down a report that claimed he refused to play second base in the minors after Michael Kay publicly retracted the story. Plus, they debate Don Mattingly's future with the Phillies and whether a deep postseason run should earn him the full-time manager's job.

entry.title: Shohei Ohtani Is Building A One-Of-A-Kind Legacy
entry.summary: A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun react to Shohei Ohtani becoming the first Japanese-born player to reach 300 career home runs and discuss what the latest milestone means for his incredible legacy. The guys explain why Ohtani's combination of elite power and pitching makes his accomplishments unlike anything baseball has ever seen, debate whether he has a realistic shot at winning both MVP and Cy Young this season, and look ahead to whether 500 home runs is within reach. Plus, they compare Ohtani's long-term outlook to other sluggers and discuss how his career could evolve once he's done pitching.

entry.title: Debating The Marlins Decision To Pull Eury Pérez During A Perfect Game Bid
entry.summary:
A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun react to the Miami Marlins pulling Eury Pérez after seven perfect innings and just 92 pitches against the Reds. Was it the right long-term decision, or did the Marlins take away a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to complete a perfect game? The guys debate modern pitching limits, player development, and why A.J. believes moments like this are simply too special to let slip away.

entry.title: Why Returning To Face Your Former Team Is So Much Harder Than Fans Realize
entry.summary:
Scott Braun, Jason Kipnis and Erik Kratz discuss the emotions that come with facing a former team after Bo Bichette's return to Toronto sparked plenty of conversation around the league. The guys share their own experiences returning to old ballparks, debate whether fans should boo departing stars, and explain why front offices, not players, often deserve the blame when franchise icons leave. Plus, they react to stories involving Kevin Gausman, Framber Valdez and Bryce Harper, and discuss why social media doesn't always reflect how fans truly feel.

entry.title: Willson Contreras' Helmet Tap Led To An Automatic Ejection?!
entry.summary:
Scott Braun, Jason Kipnis and Erik Kratz react to Willson Contreras' controversial ejection and debate whether MLB umpires have become too quick to throw players out of games. The guys discuss why the helmet tap shouldn't have resulted in an automatic ejection, whether umpires need thicker skin, and if MLB should rethink the rule moving forward. Plus, they break down Contreras' emotional postgame comments, the impact ejections like this have on the game, and why players, not umpires, should be the focus of baseball.

entry.title: Sam Blum Explains What's Next For The Angels After Firing Perry Minasian
entry.summary:
The Athletic's Sam Blum joins Foul Territory to break down the Angels' surprising decision to fire GM Perry Minasian and what it means for the franchise moving forward. Sam explains why the timing wasn't as shocking as it seemed, what role new leadership could play in reshaping the organization, and whether the Angels are finally ready to give their baseball operations department real autonomy. Plus, he discusses the club's biggest trade deadline decisions, why players like Reid Detmers and José Soriano could draw major interest, and what the next GM must do to finally turn the Angels into a sustainable winner.

entry.title: Former Mets Hitting Coach Eric Chavez Sounds Off On David Stearns' Mets
entry.summary:
Former Mets hitting coach Eric Chavez joins Foul Territory to explain why he believes Carlos Mendoza shouldn't have been blamed for New York's disappointing season. Chavez discusses David Stearns' roster construction, why managers and coaches are taking the fall for front office decisions, and why he believes the game has leaned too heavily on analytics at the expense of experienced baseball people. Plus, he shares what really happened behind the scenes with Juan Soto, explains why the Mets should become sellers at the trade deadline, and offers his perspective on the future of the organization.

entry.title: Red Sox Sweep Yankees & Anthony Rizzo Calls Out Jazz Chisholm
entry.summary:
Scott Braun, Erik Kratz and Kevin Pillar react to the Red Sox completing a stunning four-game sweep of the Yankees and discuss whether Boston's dominant starting pitching is changing the outlook of the AL East. The guys also weigh in on Jazz Chisholm Jr.'s ejection, Anthony Rizzo calling out his immaturity, and why moments like these can impact a player's reputation around the league. Plus, they discuss Marcelo Mayer's injury, the growing emphasis on bat speed throughout baseball, and whether teams are pushing young hitters too far in pursuit of more power.

entry.title: Why David Stearns Brewers Blueprint Isn't Working With The Mets
entry.summary:
The Athletic's Tyler Kepner joins Scott Braun, Erik Kratz and Kevin Pillar to explain why the Mets can't simply copy the Brewers blueprint for success. Tyler breaks down the challenges of building a winner in a big market, why spending more money doesn't automatically lead to better decisions, and what separates organizations like the Brewers and Dodgers from the rest of baseball. Plus, the guys discuss David Stearns' long-term vision for the Mets, why player development and organizational philosophy matter as much as payroll, and whether New York is on the right path despite a disappointing season.

entry.title: Reacting To MLB Players Anonymous Poll On The League's Best & Worst Organizations
entry.summary:
Scott Braun, Erik Kratz and Todd Frazier react to a new anonymous player poll revealing the best and worst organizations to play for across Major League Baseball. The guys discuss why the Dodgers, Yankees and Blue Jays continue to attract players, why teams like the Rockies, Pirates and A's received poor marks, and how seemingly small investments in player development, technology and family support can make a huge difference in free agency. Plus, Erik and Todd share firsthand stories from their playing careers about what separates the best-run organizations from the rest.
